items.headline: USMNT's knockout run starts tonight vs. Bosnia
items.body: Win or go home, and it's on home soil. The U.S. begin the World Cup knockouts tonight against Bosnia & Herzegovina at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara (8pm ET). Pochettino's side cruised through Group D and rested starters in a dead-rubber loss to Türkiye — now Pulisic and the first-choice XI are back and the real tournament begins.
items.say: The U.S. play their first World Cup knockout game tonight — Bosnia, just outside San Francisco. Win and they're through, lose and they're out. You watching?

links.text: Yahoo Sports: USMNT face Bosnia in Round of 32

items.headline: Mbappé bags a brace as France cruise past Sweden
items.body: France look every bit the favourites. Kylian Mbappé struck twice — the second a gorgeous curler into the far corner — while Michael Olise set up both his goal and Bradley Barcola's in a comfortable 3-0 win. Mbappé's now up to 18 career World Cup goals, closing in on Messi's all-time record, and France roll into the Round of 16 to meet surprise package Paraguay in Philadelphia on Saturday.
items.say: Mbappé scored twice and France looked scary — beat Sweden 3-0 to make the last 16. He's now creeping up on Messi's all-time World Cup goals record.

links.text: FOX Sports: France vs. Sweden highlights

items.headline: England meet debutants DR Congo — Kane chasing more history
items.body: England topped their group and face DR Congo in Atlanta today, a first-ever meeting between the two. Harry Kane already became England's all-time leading World Cup scorer in the group stage, and Jude Bellingham has been buzzing — but DR Congo are into the knockouts for the very first time and won't be pushovers.
items.say: England play DR Congo in the World Cup today — Harry Kane's already their all-time top scorer at these tournaments. Congo made the knockouts for the first time ever, so it could get feisty.

links.text: Al Jazeera: England vs. DR Congo preview

items.headline: Wimbledon shocker: top American Ben Shelton bounced in round one
items.body: The grass giveth chaos. Fourth seed Ben Shelton — the top-ranked American man in the draw — was stunned by Finnish qualifier Otto Virtanen in a five-set thriller, even after edging in front late in the deciding tiebreak. It's the biggest upset of the tournament so far, and comfortably the biggest win of Virtanen's career.
items.say: Big Wimbledon upset — Ben Shelton, the top American guy, got knocked out in the first round by a qualifier, and he was ahead late in the final-set tiebreak. Brutal way to lose.

links.text: Yahoo Sports: Shelton falls to Virtanen

items.headline: The Tour de France rolls out of Barcelona on Saturday
items.body: Cycling's biggest race begins Saturday with its first-ever Grand Départ in Barcelona, before three weeks and 3,300+ km wind their way to Paris. Tadej Pogačar is the runaway favourite, chasing a record-equalling fifth title, with two-time winner Jonas Vingegaard his main threat — and two summit finishes on the legendary Alpe d'Huez waiting near the end.
items.say: Tour de France kicks off Saturday — starting in Barcelona for the first time ever. Pogačar's going for a fifth win. Perfect background watching for a lazy summer weekend.

links.text: NBC Sports: 2026 Tour de France preview

items.headline: LeBron is leaving the Lakers — and the league's holding its breath
items.body: The biggest domino in free agency is on the move: LeBron James reportedly told the Lakers he won't be back for a ninth season in L.A. The Cavaliers, Warriors and Heat are the names being floated for a stunning next chapter — and yes, a homecoming to Cleveland is very much in the conversation. Everyone's waiting to see where the King lands.
items.say: LeBron reportedly told the Lakers he's leaving — people are even talking about a Cavs homecoming. Wild to think about where he ends up. Where's your bet?

links.text: CBS Sports: free agency live updates
Yahoo Sports: latest on LeBron's decision

items.headline: 'Young Washington' storms into theaters for the Fourth
items.body: Perfect timing for America's 250th: Angel Studios' historical war epic 'Young Washington' opens Friday, following a young George Washington long before the presidency — a soldier whose single misstep helps spark a global war. Ben Kingsley, Andy Serkis, Kelsey Grammer and Mary-Louise Parker fill out the cast, with Jon Erwin ('Jesus Revolution') directing.
items.say: There's a Young Washington movie opening for the Fourth — George Washington before he was, y'know, George Washington. Feels very on-theme for the 250th.

items.headline: Ella Langley's 'Choosin' Texas' is the song of the summer
items.body: Country's breakout star has the year's biggest hit. Ella Langley's 'Choosin' Texas' reclaimed No. 1 on the Hot 100 for the July 4 week and is now the longest-running chart-topper of 2026 with 11 weeks on top. The video's a whole party, too — Luke Grimes, Miranda Lambert and Kaitlin Butts all turn up.
items.say: Ella Langley's 'Choosin' Texas' is basically the song of the summer — the longest-running No. 1 of the year. If you've been to a cookout lately, you've definitely heard it.

items.headline: America turns 250 — the biggest Fourth of July ever
items.body: This isn't just any Fourth. Saturday marks 250 years since the Declaration of Independence, and the country is throwing the largest synchronized July 4 in its history. Highlights: a star-packed 'America's Block Party' in L.A. (Chris Stapleton, Smashing Pumpkins and Chaka Khan, hosted by Queen Latifah), a first-ever Times Square ball drop on July 3, and a huge 'Salute to America' fireworks show on the National Mall.
items.say: It's not just the Fourth — it's America's 250th birthday this weekend, so the celebrations are next-level. There's even a Times Square ball drop for it. Any big plans?

links.text: CNN: unique ways to celebrate the 250th
